steps.title: Find the key link to non-visual communication
steps.content:
The researchers’ claim hinges on giraffes using sounds when visual signaling is impossible.

The passage explicitly connects the humming sounds to situations where visibility is blocked.

This makes any finding that directly ties the sounds to conditions preventing visual communication the strongest support.

steps.title: Match the evidence to the claim
steps.content:
Choice B shows humming occurs only at night when visibility is low, directly linking the sound to the absence of visual signals.

The other choices either are irrelevant (A), weaken the claim (C), or provide unrelated comparisons (D).

That matches choice B — B is correct.
